mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held above 5% represents a restrictive monetary stance that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing a persistent drag on S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean forward return during FEDFUNDS>5% periods is not significantly negative (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window historically marks the early stage of a labor market downturn (Sahm-rule-like dynamic) that precedes broader earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical triggers, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or Rank IC conditional on the trigger is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the nominal federal funds rate substantially exceeds trailing CPI-based inflation, the implied real policy rate is restrictive, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; this effect is not captured by either series alone but by their joint level.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPI YoY change) against forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows, or high real-rate periods show non-negative mean forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts occurring during a late-cycle expansion signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and foreshadows a broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; the same decline in early expansion is not predictive because it reflects normalization rather than contraction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% over 3 months while GDPC1 growth is positive but decelerating
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns following the trigger are not significantly negative relative to unconditional baseline (p>0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one out of three late-cycle episodes tested.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending, a key driver of corporate revenue and earnings, and is associated with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS trailing growth and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean forward return is not significantly positive (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate an emerging manufacturing-sector contraction that leads broader corporate earnings weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Following the two-month decline trigger, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC conditional on trigger is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive payroll growth and a falling unemployment rate jointly signal a strengthening labor market that supports consumer income and spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; neither series alone captures this reinforcing dynamic since payroll growth can occur with rising unemployment (labor force expansion) without the same bullish signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint payroll-growth/unemployment-decline signal against 60d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint-improvement condition shows no significant return premium versus payroll growth alone.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve cuts rates while the unemployment rate is simultaneously elevated and rising, markets interpret the easing as confirmation of recession risk rather than stimulus, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week despite the accommodative policy move.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreases month-over-month while UNRATE > 4.5% and rising
failure_condition: Forward 5d SPX returns following simultaneous rate cuts and elevated/rising unemployment are not significantly negative relative to baseline easing periods (p>0.05), or positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.
